item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm really not a fan of the plain descriptions of events in this, without anything to really spice it up or serve as a breath of fresh air. Essentially, this is too disconnected from the actual environment it describes to evoke any real emotions.

Some form of log to put some part of this into a format where there's some sort of character or event described in a slightly smaller scale to allow us to understand would have worked wonders here, but as-is I'm not particularly enthused by reading about the politics of civilisations that I've been given no reason to care about.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In the description:

SCP-4516-A possesses a noticeably elevated thaumaturgical inclination than Earth, making spells and naturally occuring anomalous abilities much stronger in SCP-4516-D than on baseline Earth. The reverse is also true, as organisms and spells native to SCP-4516-D have less thaumaturgical prowess on Earth than they do in SCP-4516-A.

SCP-4516-D is never defined.

Upon interrogation, SCP-4516-B was discovered, in addition to SCP-4516-1, SCP-4516-3, and SCP-4516-4 based on Foundation records of similar incidents. The entities, collectively classified as SCP-4516-1,

SCP-4516-B is never defined. Also, SCP-4516-1 gets a double meaning: The first gateway and the inhabitants of SCP-4516.

Novote. There are too many gateways / portals to somewhere far away for me to like this, but apart from the lapses above this is pretty well written.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Bit late, but surface area, in this case, is best described as "elbow room". You can only fit a certain amount of individuals into a room before they start complaining about feeling crowded or having other issues.

Similarily, if you have too many species in one area, they'll fight and some will go extinct. In this case, there's not enough species to adequately fill the internal surface area of the SCP, assuming that Earth's estimated biodiversity (8.1 million species) is typical for its surface area. It'd be the equivalent of going to a very popular night club and finding only a handful of people inside.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What would be really cool is if somebody with a background in evo biology or a related field came up with some convoluted explanation for the why there'd be less biodiversity and make it part of the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm…still confused. If "enough" is being comparable to earth, how can surface area influence it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I legitimately intended to link that second one and forgot about it while messing with calculators. I'm glad someone else added it in.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you. Yes, that's what I meant.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think Nowhere Man is saying that for as massive as the surface area is, the life in it was not diverse enough when proportionally scaled with Earth.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm confused. How would there being more surface area make the biosphere less diverse?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Writers cannot do math or writers have no sense of scale probably applies here.

Maybe both?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ Super-nitpicky criticism
- Super-nitpicky criticism

It is estimated that the Dyson Sphere has a roughly 16 light minute (287,800,000 Kilometer) diameter… SCP-4516-A possesses an extremely extensive and varied biosphere, with an estimated 261 million indigenous species.

If I'm doing the math right, a sphere with a diameter of ~288 million kilometers has a surface area of ~260 quadrillion square kilometers. The Earth has a surface area of ~510 million kilometers, so SCP-4516-A has a surface area of more than 500 million times that of Earth.

Earth is estimated to have 8.7 million living species on it, so SCP-4516-A has 30 times as many indigenous species as Earth. That means the biosphere of SCP-4516-A is several million times LESS varied than that of Earth, in terms of the space those organisms have to spread and diversify.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I enjoyed the detail about naturalizing refugees from the sphere. It's a small detail, but I think the clash between the fantastic nature of wizard star population and the mundanity of applying for a green card is the kind of thing that exemplifies the spirit of the SCP wiki,

Minor scifi nitpick: in addition to the artificial gravity thing, the foundation should note the fact a dyson sphere is able to maintain stability is itself pretty anomalous. Personally I'd ditch the dyson and go with a more "realistic" megastructure.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is great!!! It's like https://tvtropes.org/pmwiki/pmwiki.php/Main/KnightTemplar but with cats. It quite literally made me laugh out loud while reading this. Keep up the good work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ Critique
- Close

unless 3/4ths of the O5 Council are in agreement.

Wouldn't it be simpler to say "without majority approval from the O5 Council?"

The description says SCP-4516 refers to four gateways, establishing SCP-4516-D as the gateway in a forest in the Dyson sphere, but then continually refers to the interior of the Dyson sphere as SCP-4516-D. Either give the Dyson sphere a separate designation, or just call it a Dyson sphere.

a handful of deities

This may just be nit-picky and down to personal tastes, but wouldn't the Foundation call them something like "Class-IV reality bending entities" or "Pistiphage Entities?"

The 'Discovery' section repeatedly uses present tense when it should be past tense.

until an identical event occurred on July 9, 2016, this time in North Korea.

You can just say, "in North Korea."

containment is established with a single fatality.

This may be personal preferences again, but shouldn't you say "with only a single fatality?"

What do SCP-4516-1 look like? Are they the same as the beings first encountered in the Bahamas?

whom is responsible

Change this to "who was responsible."

- Close

I agree with Varaxous. This needs to be expanded on because "alien environment with magical creatures and waring civilizations filled with wizards" is a really cool concept that can be a lot of fun to read and write about. Novote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This SCP maybe one of my favorites as it reminds me of the anime gate, and concept of multiple entry points into the scp is ingenius.

keep up the good work! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is, in my eyes, concept art. It's really cool, looks awesome, but ultimately it doesn't have enough depth or expansion on that cool factor.

"Wizard-planet with wizard-people and also the planet is a dyson sphere" is an amazing concept which you desperately need to expand on.

Tentative +1, but I really want to see this do more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: One of the coolest ideas I've read in a bit. I really hope you make some expansions to it or some more SCPs which are present inside the sphere! Maybe an MTF is formed to combat the cats-o-death? Also some explanation in how the cats beat the very technologically advanced would be much appreciated. Nontheless +1 !

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good post, but the first paragraph of the description mentions 4 gates when only 3 locations were given.

item.title: Really enjoyed it!
item.content:encoded:
It’s not my favorite per day but I enjoyed the article. It may be because I’m running a tabletop game with some similar story beats but that’s debatable.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
[…] state within SCP-1516, and were […]

u missed one

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There are multiple cases where, instead of SCP-4516, it says SCP-3516 and SCP-1516, both slots that are already filled. It is only called SCP-4516 once and that’s at the beginning of the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The designation keeps changing, from 4516, to 3516, to 1516.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This idea hit me last night so I figured I'd give it a shot! Lemme know what you think!
